topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

I found the login details tucked inside an old, forgotten charging cable. My hands started trembling as I typed them into the browser. The feed that populated the screen wasn't just casual posts; it was a carefully curated gallery of anonymous critiques and deeply personal, unfiltered moments my daughter had never shown me. A cold knot tightened in my stomach. I leaned back from the monitor, the chair scraping loudly against the hardwood floor. My breath hitched in my chest, and I couldn't move to close the window. The sheer scope of what I was seeing felt like a violation, a hidden world thriving just beyond my awareness.

[story 2]

The notification popped up on my phone while I was making coffee—a cryptic message from an unknown contact. Curiosity, sharp and unwelcome, pulled me to check. It led me to a profile picture that was undeniably my sixteen-year-old. Every photo was staged, every caption dripping with a performative sadness that felt profoundly wrong. I dropped the phone onto the counter. My jaw clenched so tightly I could feel the muscles in my neck strain. I paced the kitchen, running a hand through my hair repeatedly, trying to process the disconnect between the bright, smiling girl I knew and this shadowy, carefully constructed persona.

[story 3]

It was a late Tuesday night, and I stumbled upon the password while searching for a misplaced library book. When the profile loaded, the images were jarring—a collection of intensely private, unguarded moments juxtaposed with aggressively polished, public declarations. I stood frozen, the air suddenly thick and hard to draw in. My vision tunneled slightly. I backed away from the screen as if it were radiating heat. A wave of profound unease washed over me, leaving a hollow, nauseating feeling in my chest. I couldn't speak; the silence in the room amplified the frantic pounding of my own pulse.
